app.name: MQTT CLI
app.one_liner: A command-line interface for connecting to MQTT brokers supporting MQTT 5.0 and 3.1.1.

screens.name: Main Terminal Interface
screens.purpose: Provide access to all MQTT CLI commands via the terminal.

rust_commands.name: enter_shell
rust_commands.purpose: Launch an interactive MQTT shell for advanced operations.

rust_commands.name: publish_message
rust_commands.purpose: Publish a message to a specified MQTT topic on a given broker.

rust_commands.name: run_test
rust_commands.purpose: Execute tests against an MQTT broker to assess its capabilities and limitations.

rust_commands.name: subscribe_topic
rust_commands.purpose: Subscribe to one or more MQTT topics and receive messages in real time.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add support for MQTT 3.1.1 and 5.0 protocols
week 1: Implement core publish and subscribe commands
week 1: Set up basic connection management
acceptance_tests: Confirm that `mqtt sub -t test -h localhost` receives published messages in real time.
Ensure `mqtt sh` launches an interactive shell with command history.
Validate that `mqtt test -h localhost` executes a suite of broker compatibility checks.
Verify that `mqtt pub -t test -m 'hello' -h localhost` sends a message to the correct topic.
